commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Needs human handling for contributor proof and native PDF cap compatibility acceptance; there is no narrow automated repair to queue right now.; Cleared: Security review cleared: the diff adds no dependency, workflow, credential, or new network target and narrows provider-response OOM exposure. (sha=1930ec605ee254bee990a9ac17fe8c2834ba7b42)
commands.review_summary:
The PR replaces the Anthropic and Gemini native PDF success-response `res.json()` calls with the shared bounded provider JSON reader and adds local HTTP-server regression coverage for under-cap and oversized responses.

PR surface: Source +1, Tests +238. Total +239 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main has two successful native PDF provider paths that call `res.json()`, and Node's fetch implementation fully reads the body before JSON parsing; a local streaming HTTP response can exercise the oversized-body path.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs human handling for contributor proof and native PDF cap compatibility acceptance; there is no narrow automated repair to queue right now.

target.title: fix(pdf): bound AI provider response reads in PDF text extraction
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`extractTextWithAnthropic` (`anthropicAnalyzePdf`, L96) and `extractTextWithGemini` (`geminiAnalyzePdf`, L184) in `src/agents/tools/pdf-native-providers.ts` called bare `res.json()` with no size guard on successful responses. Both functions use external AI provider APIs (Anthropic Messages API and Gemini generateContent API) to extract the full text of a PDF document. For multi-page documents, the provider response can reach tens of megabytes — an unbounded `res.json()` buffers the entire response body in memory before parsing, creating an OOM risk.

## Evidence

Local terminal output after fix — `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/pdf-native-providers.test.ts --reporter=verbose`:

```
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.8 /media/vdc/0668001470/workSpace/claw-campaign/openclaw

 ✓ |agents| src/agents/tools/pdf-native-providers.test.ts > native PDF provider API calls > anthropicAnalyzePdf sends correct request shape 120ms
 ✓ |agents| src/agents/tools/pdf-native-providers.test.ts > native PDF provider API calls > anthropicAnalyzePdf honors ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL when no base URL is configured 2ms
 ✓ |agents| src/agents/tools/pdf-native-providers.test.ts > native PDF provider API calls > anthropicAnalyzePdf throws on API error 3ms
 ✓ |agents| src/agents/tools/pdf-native-providers.test.ts > native PDF provider API calls > bounds large Anthropic API error bodies 3ms
 ✓ |agents| src/agents/tools/pdf-native-providers.test.ts > native PDF provider API calls > cancels Anthropic API error bodies that exactly fill the byte cap 3ms
 ✓ |agents| src/agents/tools/pdf-native-providers.test.ts > native PDF provider API calls > anthropicAnalyzePdf throws when response has no text 2ms
 ✓ |agents| src/agents/tools/pdf-native-providers.test.ts > native PDF provider API calls > geminiAnalyzePdf sends correct request shape 3ms
 ✓ |agents| src/agents/tools/pdf-native-providers.test.ts > native PDF provider API calls > geminiAnalyzePdf throws on API error 1ms
 ✓ |agents| src/agents/tools/pdf-native-providers.test.ts > native PDF provider API calls > geminiAnalyzePdf throws when no candidates returned 2ms
 ✓ |agents| src/agents/tools/pdf-native-providers.test.ts > native PDF provider API calls > anthropicAnalyzePdf supports multiple PDFs 2ms
 ✓ |agents| src/agents/tools/pdf-native-providers.test.ts > native PDF provider API calls > anthropicAnalyzePdf uses custom base URL 2ms
 ✓ |agents| src/agents/tools/pdf-native-providers.test.ts > native PDF provider API calls > anthropicAnalyzePdf requires apiKey 1ms
 ✓ |agents| src/agents/tools/pdf-native-providers.test.ts > native PDF provider API calls > geminiAnalyzePdf requires apiKey 1ms
 ✓ |agents| src/agents/tools/pdf-native-providers.test.ts > native PDF provider API calls > geminiAnalyzePdf does not duplicate /v1beta when baseUrl already includes it 2ms
 ✓ |agents| src/agents/tools/pdf-native-providers.test.ts > native PDF provider API calls > geminiAnalyzePdf normalizes bare Google API hosts to a single /v1beta root 3ms
 ✓ |agents| src/agents/tools/pdf-native-providers.test.ts > anthropicAnalyzePdf — 16 MiB response-size guard (real HTTP server) > returns extracted text for a well-formed response under the cap 98ms
 ✓ |agents| src/agents/tools/pdf-native-providers.test.ts > anthropicAnalyzePdf — 16 MiB response-size guard (real HTTP server) > rejects oversized AI responses before buffering all 20 MiB (OOM guard) 126ms
 ✓ |agents| src/agents/tools/pdf-native-providers.test.ts > geminiAnalyzePdf — 16 MiB response-size guard (real HTTP server) > returns extracted text for a well-formed response under the cap 9ms
 ✓ |agents| src/agents/tools/pdf-native-providers.test.ts > geminiAnalyzePdf — 16 MiB response-size guard (real HTTP server) > rejects oversized AI responses before buffering all 20 MiB (OOM guard) 100ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  19 passed (19)
   Start at  15:34:16
   Duration  2.96s (transform 1.07s, setup 520ms, import 1.68s, tests 489ms, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 14.33s
```

Key integration tests (driven by a real `node:http` server — no fetch mocking):

- **under-cap**: server returns a valid JSON body → function parses and returns the extracted text correctly ✓
- **over-cap (OOM guard)**: server streams 20 × 1 MiB chunks (20 MiB total, no `Content-Length`). `readProviderJsonResponse` rejects with `/JSON response exceeds/` and cancels the stream before the full body is buffered ✓

## Summary

- `anthropicAnalyzePdf` (L96) and `geminiAnalyzePdf` (L184) in `src/agents/tools/pdf-native-providers.ts` called bare `res.json()` with no size limit on success responses. AI models return the full extracted text of every PDF page in a single JSON body; multi-page documents can reach tens of megabytes and exhaust process memory.
- Both calls are replaced with `readProviderJsonResponse()` from `../provider-http-errors.js`, which caps response bodies at the shared 16 MiB default (`PROVIDER_JSON_RESPONSE_MAX_BYTES`) before attempting JSON parsing.
- Existing mock-based tests updated to use real `Response` objects (which expose a readable body stream) since `readProviderJsonResponse` reads the stream directly instead of calling `.json()`.
- Four new integration tests added — driven by a real `node:http` server, no fetch mocking — proving the guard fires before the body is fully buffered.

## Validation Evidence

```
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.8 /media/vdc/0668001470/workSpace/claw-campaign/openclaw

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  19 passed (19)
   Start at  14:49:55
   Duration  3.98s (transform 1.94s, setup 490ms, import 2.58s, tests 571ms, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 16.78s
```

Key tests (`anthropicAnalyzePdf — 16 MiB response-size guard (real HTTP server)` and `geminiAnalyzePdf — 16 MiB response-size guard (real HTTP server)`):
- **under-cap**: real server returns a valid JSON body → function parses and returns the text correctly.
- **over-cap (OOM guard)**: real server streams 20 × 1 MiB chunks (20 MiB total). The test asserts the error message matches `/JSON response exceeds/` **and** that `chunksWritten < 20`, proving the body stream is cancelled before being fully buffered.

## Security & Privacy

No new network targets introduced. The change narrows the OOM attack surface: a malicious or misbehaving AI endpoint can no longer force the process to buffer an arbitrarily large response body.

## Compatibility

Behaviour is fail-closed: responses that exceed 16 MiB now throw an error instead of silently consuming unbounded memory. Normal AI provider responses for PDF extraction are well under the cap (max_tokens defaults to 4096 ≈ ~16 KiB of JSON).
target.author: alix-007
